description:
Terminal phase of bl-f021. Cuts the 0.5.0 release on crates.io + GitHub, with all prior phases' evidence as the readiness signal. After this ball closes, the epic itself closes (no `gates` link, but its phasing rationale 'each child carries its own conformance tests; bl-8a9a → bl-e609 demonstrated this works' is satisfied — every phase has shipped).

Depends on Phase 9 (bl-1482) so that the inter-version upgrade matrix is proven before the version that locks the matrix in is published.

## Scope

### Version bump

- `Cargo.toml`: `0.4.x` → `0.5.0`. Per project_release_plz_marker_self_match memory, ensure the bump justification in the bump commit body contains `[minor]` *inside backticks* if quoting the marker — bare `[minor]` in prose self-matches release-plz's regex and forces hand-PR-edits.

### release-plz handshake

- After bumping Cargo.toml, push to main (via normal ball workflow — `bl review` + `bl close` from this phase's worktree).
- release-plz opens its PR; review the proposed `CHANGELOG.md` against the per project_changelog_auto memory (rich commit bodies should give release-plz good material).
- Cross-reference release-plz's draft against the human-frame `docs/RELEASE-NOTES-0.5.0.md` from Phase 7 — they should agree on the headline ('XDG three-file layout; min_bl_version bump; …').
- Per project_release_pr_merge_no_trigger memory: merge the release PR; if CI doesn't fire automatically after merge, push an empty `balls:`-prefixed commit to trigger the publish workflow.

### Publish verification

- crates.io: `bl` 0.5.0 visible, downloadable, `cargo install bl --version 0.5.0` from a clean tmpdir succeeds.
- GitHub release: tag exists, release notes attached, binary artifacts (if produced by the workflow) present.
- balls-github-plugin's compatibility: confirm the plugin's pinned bl version (or compatible-range) still resolves; if a plugin update is needed, file a follow-up ball — *don't* widen this ball's scope to include plugin work.

### Post-publish dogfood

- One final `make install` from main on the dev machine to confirm 0.5.0 self-installs.
- One `bl prime` round-trip against the live published tracker to confirm the version-floor warning surfaces correctly against any clone running pre-0.5.0 (link evidence to Phase 9's matrix).

### Close the epic

- After publish verified, `bl close` this phase, then `bl close` bl-f021. The epic's final commit message references all ten phases.

## Exit criteria

- 0.5.0 live on crates.io.
- GitHub release published with notes.
- Local install + prime confirms self-consistency.
- bl-f021 (and all children) closed.

## Why a discrete ball, not folded onto Phase 9

The release ceremony has its own failure modes (release-plz PR formatting, marker regex, publish-workflow non-trigger) recorded in the project memories. Folding it into Phase 9 would mix 'inter-version proof' with 'release plumbing' — two unrelated skill sets, two unrelated rollback shapes.

## Risks

- Per project_release_pr_merge_no_trigger memory: `gh pr merge` of a release-plz PR can land without firing CI. Mitigation already named: empty `balls:`-prefixed commit to trigger. Watch for it; don't assume CI ran.
- Per project_release_plz_marker_self_match memory: `[minor]` in prose self-matches the regex. Wrap markers in backticks or hand-edit the PR via REST PATCH (`gh pr edit` is broken per the memory).
- 0.5.0 is a minor bump on a 0.x crate, which has wider semver latitude than 1.x — but the bumped `min_bl_version` is the real breaking-change story. Release notes should call this out explicitly; the version number alone won't signal it.
title: Phase 10: cut and publish 0.5.0
